audit_list: C2 challenge profile inherited exactly from Iteration 4
B1 remains localized partition evidence only
B2 reproduces Iteration 4 partial result exactly
B3 unlocked only after B2 C0/C1/C2 evidence is present
B3 improves the same B2 C2 leakage/support/coherence failures
B4 x C2 remains provisional and does not close B4 x C5
B4 retention distinguished from coupling, neighbor leakage, and merge pressure
Iteration 6 guardrails recorded for B3_C4 and B4_C5
all boundary claims and final AP6 remain false

b3_improvement_audit.claim_boundary: B3 support is artifact-level flux repair candidate evidence, not agency, intention, selfhood, native support, or final AP6

b3_unlock_audit.unlock_reason: Iteration 4 already evaluated B2 under C0, C1, and C2, so Iteration 5 may run B3 x C2 without hiding unsupported persistence

b4_c2_provisional_audit.claim_boundary: B4 x C2 is a flux stress row only; B4 x C5 remains required for shared-medium separability

c2_fixed_policy.perturbation_present_semantics: C2 is directional flux through a coupling channel; it is not unstructured perturbation
c2_fixed_policy.policy: C2 pressure, threshold floors, leakage ceiling, external-state role, and admissibility rules are inherited from Iteration 4 without retuning per boundary state

challenge_sweep_source_provenance.file_sha_policy: file SHA-256 is recorded for current artifact bytes; semantic provenance uses output_digest because generated_at and git metadata are excluded from the stable digest

multi_basin_merge_control.observed: B4 x C2 separates retained flux, redirected coupling flux, neighbor leakage, and merge pressure

git.head: f822df0723b2826378e19852aaf33c7b7099cb05

?? experiments/2026-06-N16-lgrc-self-environment-boundary/scripts/build_n16_boundary_state_sweep_matrix.py

iteration_6_guardrails.b3_c2_scope_guard: B3_C2 supports only C2 directional-flux repair improvement. It does not show general repair, breach reclosure, autonomous reabsorption, native support, agency, or final AP6.

b3_c4_contrast_pair.iteration_6_question: Does the B3 mechanism that repaired C2 leakage also support breach/reclosure in B3_C4, or was it only flux-specific?

iteration_6_guardrails.b4_c2_scope_guard: B4_C2 remains partial, not nearly supported. Retained flux must not dominate interpretation while coupling, neighbor leakage, merge pressure, and basin separation remain unresolved.

b4_c5_design_inputs.iteration_6_question: Can B4_C5 resolve shared-medium separability rather than merely preserve or redistribute retained flux?

iteration_6_guardrails.claim_boundary: Iteration 6 may use Iteration 5 contrast pairs and risk signals, but final AP6 remains blocked until controls, replay, and claim classification close cleanly.

selected_probe_expectations.B0_C3: reject structured external coherence as boundary support; external coherence remains false-positive pressure, not self-region evidence
selected_probe_expectations.B1_C2: reproduce weak localized flux partial behavior; extraction remains below persistence or repair
selected_probe_expectations.B2_C1: reproduce bounded noise tolerance; C1 success must not substitute for C2/C4/C5 evidence
selected_probe_expectations.B3_C4: test whether the B3 mechanism that repaired C2 leakage also supports breach/reclosure, or whether the improvement was flux-specific
selected_probe_expectations.B4_C5: test whether B4 can resolve neighbor leakage, redirected coupling flux, merge pressure, and insufficient basin separation under shared medium pressure

maturity_gradient_summary.claim_boundary: Iteration 5 refines C2 flux-survival requirements only; final AP6 remains blocked

fixed_challenge.perturbation_present_semantics: C2 is directional flux through a coupling channel; it is not unstructured perturbation
fixed_challenge.policy: C2 pressure, threshold floors, leakage ceiling, external-state role, and admissibility rules are inherited from Iteration 4 without retuning per boundary state

necessary_flux_survival_requirements: stable fixed C2 policy and failure vocabulary
support floor preservation under directional flux
leakage at or below quiet leakage ceiling
retained flux must be separated from redirected coupling flux
B4 separability still requires B4 x C5

what_changed_relative_to_iteration_4.B1_vs_B2: B1 remains weaker than B2 under the same C2 pressure: lower retained flux, higher leakage, and no persistence claim
what_changed_relative_to_iteration_4.B3_supported_claim_blocked_note: B3 row_decision=supported means the fixed C2 failure modes are resolved at artifact level; boundary_claim_allowed and final_ap6_supported remain false
what_changed_relative_to_iteration_4.B3_vs_B4: B4 retained flux is slightly higher than B3, but B3 is the supported C2 repair row because B4 still has neighbor leakage, merge confusion pressure, leakage above the quiet ceiling, and basin separation below the shared-medium floor
what_changed_relative_to_iteration_4.B4_vs_B2: B4 improves retained flux but introduces coupling/neighbor leakage and merge pressure, so it remains partial under C2

boundary_stability_score.reason: ordered maturity score for the fixed-C2 sweep: null < localized partition < B2 partial anchor < B4 coupled partial < B3 repair candidate that resolves the specific B2 C2 failures

bounded_reabsorption_response.claim_boundary: net retained flux under B3 repair pressure; not autonomous reabsorption or native repair

flux_tolerance_score.reason: fixed-C2 comparison score for how much directional flux remains usable without violating the claim boundary

leakage_ratio.reason: leakage_ratio is a normalized failure-severity score, not direct outbound_flux / inbound_flux. B2 is inherited exactly from Iteration 4; B0/B1/B3/B4 are fixed construction anchors for B-state comparison under the same C2 profile.

row_anchors.B0: 1.0 because externally organized flux has no supported internal boundary
row_anchors.B1: 0.294 because localized partition leaks strongly under fixed C2
row_anchors.B3: 0.112 because bounded repair lowers leakage below the quiet ceiling
row_anchors.B4: 0.132 because retained flux improves but neighbor leakage/merge pressure keep leakage above ceiling

metric_construction_formulas.metric_status: Iteration 5 uses deterministic construction values for the fixed-C2 stress probe, not independent simulation outputs. Formula-derived values are marked here; construction anchors are listed explicitly.

repair_score.reason: B3-only artifact-level bounded repair/reabsorption score under C2

retained_flux.reason: retained_flux records intended-basin retained signal under fixed C2; B2 is inherited exactly from Iteration 4, while B3/B4 test repair and coupling effects without changing C2 pressure

metric_construction_rationale.b2_anchor: B2_C2 reproduces the Iteration 4 partial row exactly
metric_construction_rationale.b3_success_test: B3 must reduce the same B2 C2 leakage/support/coherence-margin failures; a different success criterion is not accepted
metric_construction_rationale.b4_caution: B4 x C2 is only a flux stress row; B4 x C5 remains required for shared-medium multi-basin separability
metric_construction_rationale.construction_type: deterministic fixed-C2 boundary-state sweep; metric values are explicit B-state stress-probe construction values anchored to the Iteration 4 B2 x C2 row

arc_method_mapping.classification_of_becoming: classify the same fixed C2 flux pressure across B0-B4, preserving the distinction between null, partition, persistence, repair, and multi-basin candidate states
arc_method_mapping.cultivation_of_becoming: cultivate reusable flux-survival requirements before selected B3 x C4 and B4 x C5 probes
arc_method_mapping.interrogation_of_becoming: ask which boundary-state maturity level changes the B2 C2 partial result and why, rather than asking whether any one row can be made to pass
arc_method_mapping.naturalization_of_becoming: treat improved flux handling as artifact-level boundary evidence only, not native support or native self/environment understanding
